_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about why the first permanent British colony was set up on Gadigal Country (Sydney) in 1788.

_source.w: We are learning about why the first permanent British colony was set up on Gadigal Country (Sydney) in 1788.
y: Understanding why colonisation happened helps us make sense of how Australia came to be the way it is today.
Learning the causes behind big historical events helps us see that history is shaped by choices and circumstances.
Knowing this history is essential for understanding the experiences of Aboriginal Peoples whose land was taken.
eg: Looking at maps of early European exploration of Australia's coastline.
Discussing what a 'colony' is and why Britain wanted to establish one far from home.
Learning about the voyages of navigators like James Cook and what they reported.
Exploring why Britain chose Botany Bay and then Sydney Cove as the site for the colony.
Discussing the role of overcrowded prisons in Britain's decision to send convicts to Australia.
Asking what the Gadigal People's life was like before 1788 and how colonisation disrupted it.
